Harvey is an old rival of Stemple's from Harvard. He defeated Harvey three times in mock trials while they were Law students, but has been avoiding confrontation with him ever since.

characters.name: Albert Chan
characters.description: Albert ChanPortrayed byIvan ShawBiographical InformationOccupationProperty DeveloperPhysical DescriptionGenderMaleCharacter InformationFirst appearanceThe Deputy MayorGALLERY (4) Albert Chanis a property developer and a business partner ofPat McGann. Albert Chan is a property developer who formed a business relationship withPat McGann. Having done work in Shanghai, he soon moved to Macau, working with the mayor there. Albert soon became an investor and partner in McGann's North Park project, and flew to Chicago to meet with McGann at a club, accompanied by a woman. Albert asks McGann how soon work could begin, and is dismayed when McGann informs him they must wait 90 days. Albert mentions McGann having the mayor in his pocket, with McGann claiming the mayor cannot do everything, only for Albert to state that the mayor in Macau can and that he should put his money there. As he leaves, he notifies McGann that he would revoke his money if the North Park project does not begin within the week.[1]

characters.name: Alex Williams
characters.description: Alex WilliamsPortrayed byDulé HillBiographical InformationResidenceNew York, New YorkNicknameLittle Man(byHarvey Specter)The A-Team(by himself)OccupationCorporate AttorneyPositionName PartnerSenior PartnerAssociate(formerly)InstitutionLitt Wheeler Williams BennettSpecter Litt Wheeler Williams(formerly)Zane Specter Litt Wheeler Williams(formerly)Zane Specter Litt(formerly)Specter Litt(formerly)Pearson Specter Litt(formerly)Bratton Gould(formerly)Physical DescriptionGenderMaleHair colorBlackEye colorBrownRelationshipsSignificant OthersRosalie Williams(wife)ChildrenJoy Williams(daughter)Debbie Williams(daughter)Character InformationFirst appearanceThe StatueLatest appearanceOne Last ConGALLERY (181) Alex Williams, J.D. is a corporate attorney and a name partner atLitt Wheeler Williams Bennett. While playing poker withHarvey Specter, Alex proposes that Harvey joinBratton Gouldand that the two of them become junior partners. WhileThomas Brattonis interesting in poaching one fromSkaddeninstead, he is willing to promote Harvey and Alex to junior partner under the condition that Harvey gives his word that he will leavePearson Hardmanto join them, which Harvey does. However, as Harvey begins to pack up his things,Jessica Pearsonapologizes if he has been mistreated and informs him that she has made a deal withDaniel Hardmanthat Harvey would be the firm's next junior partner, and Harvey decides to stay. Harvey then apologizes to Alex for breaking his word and promises Alex that if he ever required a favor from him in the future, no matter what, he would repay him.

characters.name: Alexandra Leeds
characters.description: Alexandra LeedsPortrayed byAlicia CoppolaBiographical InformationOccupationProsecutorInstitutionU.S. Attorney General's OfficePhysical DescriptionGenderFemaleHair colorBrownEye colorBrownCharacter InformationAppears inRules of the GameGALLERY (1) Alexandra Leedsis a prosecutor who works for the U.S. Attorney General's Office, tasked with investigating District AttorneyCameron Dennisover allegations of evidence tampering. She made her sole appearance in "Rules of the Game". Alexandra Leeds is a prosecutor at the Attorney General's Office who was assigned to investigateCameron Dennisand the allegations that he was guilty of evidence tampering. Knowing that his current employees would not testify against him, Leeds subpoenaedHarvey Specter, although Harvey refused to rat on his mentor. After Harvey convinces Cameron to cut a deal, Cameron pins the blame on Harvey, claiming that Harvey was responsible for the crimes. However, using evidence given to him byDonna Paulsen,Jessica Pearsonthreatens to give Leeds and the Attorney General the damning evidence unless Cameron takes the blame for his crimes.[1]

characters.name: Amy
characters.description: AmyPortrayed byMelanie PapaliaBiographical InformationResidenceNew York, New YorkOccupationExecutive Assistant(formerly)PhD studentPositionExecutive Assistant(formerly)InstitutionSidwell Investment Group(formerly)Physical DescriptionGenderFemaleHair colorBrownEye colorDark BrownCharacter InformationFirst appearance"One-Two-Three Go..."Latest appearance"Litt the Hell Up"GALLERY (5) Amywas the personal "Donna-like" assistant ofMike Rossduring his tenure atSidwell Investment Group. Bright and quick-witted, Amy developed a nearly instant rapport with Mike, aiding him in capacities both personal and professional, ensuring that even in tough times he kept his eye on the ball. She is pursuing a PhD in psychology. Jonathan Sidwelldismissed her after he found out, she knew about Mike's agreement withCharles Forstmanto cut Sidwell out of the deal overGillis Industries.[2]

characters.name: Angela Cook
characters.description: Angela CookPortrayed byChantel RileyBiographical InformationResidenceNorth Park, Chicago, IllinoisOccupationNursing AssistantPhysical DescriptionGenderFemaleRelationshipsParentsLillian Cook(mother)ChildrenCory Cook(son)Mark Cook(son)RelativesAlan Pearson†(maternal uncle)Jessica Pearson(maternal cousin)Leah Pearson(maternal cousin)Virginia Pearson(aunt)Character InformationFirst appearanceGood-ByeLatest appearanceThe FixerGALLERY (28) Angela Cookis a nursing assistant, the daughter ofLillian Cookand a paternal cousin ofJessica Pearson. Angela is the daughter ofLillian Cookand the paternal niece ofAlan Pearson. Angela grew up around her cousin,Jessica Pearson, with the pair both living poor, Angela living in a public housing apartment with her mother in the North Park neighborhood, on Avalon and 81st Street. At some point, Alan became a successful surgeon and took his wife and children with him as they moved out of Chicago, eventually becoming wealthy. Angela continued to live in the public housing apartment with Lillian, and soon gave birth to two sons, Cory and Mark, who lived with her in the apartment as well.

characters.name: Anita Gibbs
characters.description: This article is astub. You can help Suits Wiki byexpanding it. Anita GibbsPortrayed byLeslie HopeBiographical InformationOccupationFederal ProsecutorAssistant U.S. AttorneyInstitutionU.S. Attorney's Office for the Southern District of New YorkCharacter InformationFirst appearanceBlowbackLatest appearanceFull DisclosureGALLERY (15) Anita Gibbsis an Assistant U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York and the lead prosecutor in theMike Rosscase when Mike is charged for practicing law without a license. She was on New York Bar Exam review board at the time of Mike's hearing for entering the bar. She planned to not allow Mike to become a member of the bar, butJessica Pearsonreminded her of her compassion and she allowed for Mike to become a member of the Bar.

characters.name: Anthony Mazlo
characters.description: Anthony MazloPortrayed byBruce AltmanBiographical InformationStatusAliveNicknameTonyOccupationBusinessmanPhysical DescriptionGenderMaleHair colorGrayEye colorBrownCharacter InformationAppears inIdentity CrisisGALLERY (1) Anthony Mazlois a criminal responsible for embezzling millions of dollars fromStable Shelters. Anthony Mazlo was the head of an investment firm that embezzled millions of dollars fromLucille Jackson's accounts. Initially denying the embezzlement, Mazlo attempted to portray his second-in-command,Elliot Perkins, as a scapegoat following Elliot's death. However, with the help ofLouis LittandLola Jensen,Harvey Specterwas able to obtain proof of Mazlo's crimes. In exchange for no criminal prosecution, Mazlo returned the stolen money and resigned from his position.[1]

characters.description: Anthony MorrisonPortrayed byXuan FraserBiographical InformationStatusAliveResidenceNew York CityOccupationJudgeInstitutionNew York Supreme CourtPhysical DescriptionGenderMaleRelationshipsRelativesmarriedCharacter InformationAppears in"The Shelf Life" Hon.Anthony Morrisonis a judge of New York Supreme Court and also a friend ofHarvey's. He appeared in episode "The Shelf Life", when Harvey visited him and asked for a favor. Morrison then helped Harvey by accessing and searching sealed court documents related to his investigation ofDreibach Accounting.

characters.name: Arthur Reeves
characters.description: Arthur ReevesBiographical InformationStatusUnknownOccupationLawyer(formerly)PositionManaging Partner(formerly)Name Partner(formerly)Senior Partner(formerly)Physical DescriptionGenderMaleCharacter InformationAppears inThe Statue(mentioned only) Arthur Reeves, J.D. was a lawyer who founded the law firm that later becameGordon Schmidt Van Dyke. AfterStanley Gordon, Schmidt andCharles Van Dyketook over the firm, they dropped Reeves’ largest client. Reeves was mentioned byJessica Pearsonin a flashback featured in "The Statue".

characters.name: Ava Hessington
characters.description: Ava HessingtonPortrayed byMichelle FairleyBiographical InformationStatusAliveResidenceLondonOccupationCEO of Hessington OilPositionCEOPhysical DescriptionGenderFemaleRelationshipsParentsUnnamed fatherCharacter InformationFirst appearanceThe ArrangementLatest appearanceStayGALLERY (19) Ava Hessington, PhD. is the CEO ofHessington Oilwho was being charged with bribery and the murder of her opposers byCameron Dennis. ThoughHarvey Specterwas her lawyer in court, it took plenty of resources and quite a few lawyers fromPearson Darby Specter, including all three name partners, to keep her out of jail. It was later revealed thatStephen Huntley,Edward Darby's right-hand man was responsible for the murders and he was trying to set her up for it. She later suedPearson Specterfor malpractice but later Harvey convinced her to drop the case. Ava was born to the Hessington family, who have been in the oil business for some time. Growing up, Ava knew that her father was secretly in love withEdward Darby, a close friend of the family. At the time, a lawyer and an oilman coming out of the closet would have meant career suicide, and so this remained a secret from the world. In order to be together, Ava's father and Darby would go away on vacation; when this happened, Ava would join them and pretend to be Darby's girlfriend, in order to provide cover.[1]Because of this, Darby considers Ava to be family, which is why he worked so hard to clear Ava of all charges.

characters.name: Avery McKernon
characters.description: Avery McKernonPortrayed byPeter KeleghanBiographical InformationStatusDeceasedOccupationBusinessmanCEO ofMcKernon MotorsPhysical DescriptionGenderMaleCharacter InformationAppears inFork in the RoadGALLERY (1) Avery McKernonwas the founder and CEO ofMcKernon MotorsandHarvey Specter's first client as a corporate attorney. After senior partner Fairchild dies,Louis Littcame up with the idea to team up withHarvey Specterto convinceDaniel Hardmanto promote the pair of them from senior associates to junior partners rather than promote an existing junior partner to senior partner. Hardman agreed to promote one of them, providing they were able to signMcKernon Motorsas a client.

characters.name: Benjamin
characters.description: BenjaminPortrayed byDavid RealeBiographical InformationNicknameBenZuckerbergBenson(byLouis Litt)Genius(byDonna Paulsen)Sailor(by Louis Litt)OccupationIT SpecialistPositionVice PresidentInstitutionSpecter Litt Wheeler WilliamsZane Specter Litt Wheeler Williams(formerly)Zane Specter Litt(formerly)Pearson Specter Litt(formerly)Pearson Specter(formerly)Pearson Darby Specter(formerly)Pearson Darby(formerly)Pearson Hardman(formerly)Connolly White(formerly)Physical DescriptionGenderMaleCharacter InformationFirst appearanceUndefeatedLatest appearanceIf the Shoe FitsGALLERY (17) —Benjamin andMike Ross,Undefeated Benjaminis an IT specialist and the Vice President of the IT department atSpecter Litt Wheeler Williams. As the person in charge of the law firm's entire computer and server network, Benjamin manages and repairs all the computers as well as protects the network from outside attacks. He was one of the three employees, along withDonna PaulsenandGretchen Bodinski, that did not resign fromPearson Specter LittfollowingMike Ross' conviction.

characters.name: Betsy Sullivan
characters.description: Betsy SullivanPortrayed byAbby BrammellBiographical InformationResidenceHyde Park, Chicago, IllinoisOccupationBusinesswomanPhysical DescriptionGenderFemaleCharacter InformationAppears inThe Union LeaderGALLERY (4) Betsy Sullivanis a wealthy woman who was considered byPat McGannto become alderman of the 54th ward. She made her sole appearance in "The Union Leader". Betsy Sullivan is a woman who is a friend ofPat McGann. Born in wealth, she hosted fundraisers and had her own company. Beliving that people of color were meant to serve her and were beneath her, she created loopholes that allowed prisoners and ex-convicts of Chicago to work for her, claiming they were grateful for the work, while also skirting union laws. Betsy and McGann soon conspired for her to become appointed alderman of the 54th ward in Chicago's South Side following former aldermanDeborah Coates' resignation, with Betsy intending to insert the same loophole to allow McGann to hire non-unionized labor. McGann scheduled a meeting with Chicago mayorBobby Novakto demand her appointment, although the meeting was instead taken byJessica Pearson, who demanded to have Betsy vetted first.

characters.name: Blake Everett
characters.description: Blake EverettPortrayed byStephen FriedrichBiographical InformationOccupationHead of Corporate AffairsPositionVice PresidentInstitutionIckarisPhysical DescriptionGenderMaleCharacter InformationAppears inThe Former City AttorneyGALLERY (7) Blake Everettis one of the vice presidents of Ickaris' Corporate Affairs division who made his sole appearance in "The Former City Attorney". Blake Everett, as one of the VPs of Ickaris' Corporate Affairs, was tasked with touring several US cities in order to sign a deal for Ickaris' second headquarters. Wanting Ickaris to choose Chicago,Yoli CastilloandDerrick Mayeswere tasked byJessica Pearsonand MayorBobby Novakrespectively to give Blake a tour of the city. During the tour, Yoli criticizes and insults him for spending a fortune on a new corporate building while his company's workers rely on food stamps. While Blake had heard such criticisms before, it had not been from a member of an administration attempting to court him, and leaves insulted.

While Blake had heard such criticisms before, it had not been from a member of an administration attempting to court him, and leaves insulted.

characters.description: Bobby NovakPortrayed byMorgan SpectorBiographical InformationFull nameRobert NovakResidenceChicago, IllinoisNicknameBobbyMr. MayorBobby Boy(byPat McGann)Superman(byDerrick MayesandJessica Pearson)OccupationMayor of ChicagoAlderman(formerly)Public Defender(formerly)Physical DescriptionGenderMaleRelationshipsSignificant OthersStephanie Novak(wife)Keri Allen(extramarital lover)ChildrenBobby Novak, Jr.(son)Danny Novak(son)SiblingsNick D'Amato(half-brother)Character InformationFirst appearanceGood-ByeLatest appearanceThe FixerGALLERY (65) Robert "Bobby" Novakis the mayor of Chicago and the paternal half-brother of his personal driver,Nick D'Amato. Robert Novak, commonly known as Bobby, is the mayor of Chicago and the half-brother ofNick D'Amato, as well as the lover ofKeri Allen. He is married toStephanie Novak, with whom he has two sons, Bobby Jr. and Danny.

characters.name: Brian Altman
characters.description: Brian AltmanPortrayed byJake EpsteinBiographical InformationFull nameBrian Simon AltmanResidenceNew York, New YorkNicknameMufasaBrian(byLouis Litt)B-Dawg(byKatrina Bennett)OccupationAttorneyPositionAssociateInstitutionZane Specter Litt Wheeler Williams(formerly)Zane Specter Litt(formerly)Specter Litt(formerly)Pearson Specter Litt(formerly)Physical DescriptionGenderMaleRelationshipsSignificant OthersJulie(wife)ChildrenAustin Altman(son)Character InformationFirst appearanceSkin in the GameLatest appearanceWhatever It TakesGALLERY (15) Brian Simon Altmanis a lawyer andKatrina Bennett's former associate atZane Specter Litt Wheeler Williams. He is first seen when Louis enters the bullpen to assign the associates tasks. He is urged by a fellow associate,Stephanie Patel, to ask for two days off and when pressed as to why, he reveals he is going to be a father. Louis turns him down before proceeding to yell at him.

characters.name: Bruno
characters.description: BrunoPortrayed by"Molly" of Gryphonwood Birmans (Birman cat)Biographical InformationStatusDeceasedResidenceNew York CityPhysical DescriptionGenderMaleEye colorBlueRelationshipsRelativesLouis Litt(keeper)Character InformationAppears in"Break Point" BrunowasLouis Litt'spet, breed of Birman cat. Louis brought him toPearson Hardmanoffice in episode "Break Point" and orderedHarold Gundersonto look after him for a while. Louis was thinking that Bruno had not been eating because of dental problems and took him to the vet, but it turned out that Bruno was terminally ill and died, which left Louis devastated.[1] Louis keeps framed photography of Bruno on his desk, as seen in episode "Blood in the Water" during an argument withHarvey.[2]Bruno was also mentioned byNigel Nesbittin the mock trial over the ownership of Nigel's catMikadoin episode "She's Mine".[3]

characters.name: Burt Kimball
characters.description: Burt KimballPortrayed byDoug MurrayBiographical InformationStatusAliveOccupationStock TraderInstitutionMorello Asset ManagementPhysical DescriptionGenderMaleHair colorBrownEye colorBrownCharacter InformationAppears inTricks of the TradeGALLERY (1) Burt Kimballwas the former second-in-command toDean Morelloat Morello Asset Management. In 1999, Burt Kimball was represented in a civil suit byNick Zegan, and the duo formed a partnership as a result. Over the years, Zegan would provide Kimball with insider information, and Kimball would relay the information on illegal tip sheets for the traders to use. After one trade, purchased byGabby Stone, was flagged by the DOJ, Kimball threw Gabby under the bus, arranging for Zegan to "represent" Gabby in order to ensure he did not receive any responsibility for his crime.

characters.name: Cameron Dennis
characters.description: Cameron DennisPortrayed byGary ColeBiographical InformationStatusAliveNicknameDirty Public ServantCorrupt PrickOccupationProsecutorDistrict Attorney(formerly)PositionManhattan District Attorney(formerly)InstitutionThe New York County District Attorney's Office(formerly)Physical DescriptionGenderMaleCharacter InformationFirst appearanceRules of the GameLatest appearanceThe Hand That Feeds YouHarvey(flashback)GALLERY (25) Cameron Dennisis a prosecutor and was one ofHarvey Specter's mentors along withJessica Pearson. He was the Manhattan District Attorney whom Harvey worked under as Assistant District Attorney before working atPearson Hardman. He has questionable ethics and engages in illegal acts in order to ensure that those he personally deems guilty, regardless of evidence, are incarcerated. In "Rules of the Game", Cameron is accused of and investigated for burying evidence, and Harvey is forced to testify against Cameron. Being his mentor, Harvey is unwilling to help form a case against Cameron, so Harvey asks him to just step down from his position and make a deal with the other side. When Cameron realizes that Harvey's integrity might just make him talk against him, he decides to sell out Harvey to save himself, instead. When Jessica finds out, she uses the informationDonnagave her to blackmail Cameron into backing down and resigning.[1]

characters.name: Charles Forstman
characters.description: Charles ForstmanPortrayed byEric RobertsBiographical InformationFull nameCharles ForstmanStatusIncarceratedResidenceNew YorkOccupationBusinessmanHedge Fund OwnerPhysical DescriptionGenderMaleCharacter InformationFirst appearanceLeveragedLatest appearancePrisoner's DilemmaGALLERY (8) Charles Forstmanis a billionaire with a bad reputation of being a shady and manipulative banker. Not much is known of Charles Forstman's early life, but his business practices earned him a reputation of being very shady, having engaged in illegal activities. He had a deal withHarvey Specter's father once, a deal both Harvey and his younger brotherMarcuswere aware of. When Harvey won a case as the Assistant District Attorney, he was approached by Forstman who offered him a job, but Harvey refused based on his bad reputation, a reputation thatDonna Paulsenwas easily able to find out about online and one thatJessica Pearsonwas aware of as well.
